action.hypothesis: JumpReLU at K=128 may be paying unnecessary reconstruction cost to the auxiliary dead-feature objective, and removing auxk pressure could lower primary FVU below the current 0.1724 full frontier.
action.summary: Hold the improved JumpReLU K=128 setup fixed at LR=1.6e-3 and start the auxk sweep by setting AUXK_ALPHA=0 to test whether pure reconstruction training beats the current anchor.
action.self_review: The LR sweep already found a clear improvement and 3.2e-3 only gave a marginal archive result, so shifting to auxk is the cleanest next one-variable probe. The main risk is increased dead features or weaker long-run stability, but proxy is the right stage to test whether the auxiliary term is helping or hurting frontier quality.
notes_to_memory: LR=3.2e-3 slightly improved over 1.6e-3 in full FVU but only marginally, so optimization gains from LR alone appear to be flattening.
Round 14 begins the requested AUXK sweep on the strongest K=128 JumpReLU anchor rather than continuing ever-higher LR exploration.
This round tests whether the auxiliary dead-feature objective is now constraining primary reconstruction quality at the improved learning rate regime.
next_hypotheses: If AUXK_ALPHA=0 improves proxy FVU, promote to full and then refine auxk near zero, such as 0.01, on the same K=128 anchor.
If AUXK_ALPHA=0 is worse, restore auxk and test AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 next to probe whether a smaller but nonzero auxiliary weight is optimal.
If auxk changes do not improve quality, keep K=128 fixed and start expansion-factor sweeps, beginning with EXPANSION_FACTOR=16.
